hypothesis: If a stereochemistry-aware diffusion model (LoQI) is trained on a large-scale dataset of neural-network-potential-optimized conformers (ChEMBL3D), then it will generate low-energy molecular conformers with near-quantum-mechanical accuracy that are competitive with or superior to classical conformer generation methods in terms of energy, RMSD to reference structures, and stereochemical fidelity, because exposure to diverse, high-quality conformational data combined with explicit stereochemical graph augmentations enables the model to learn the underlying energy landscape of drug-like molecules.
